experiment: FM-19 logical overwrite: collision surface measurement + structural hardening
prior_work: L-525 (two-layer concurrent safety), L-601 (structural enforcement theorem), L-931 (close_lane.py guard), L-947 (epistemological failure migration). FM-19 identified S420 as CRITICAL UNMITIGATED (29% collision rate, 0 automated defenses).
method: Git log analysis of last 50/100 commits for concurrent file modifications within 5-commit windows. Contention classified by write mode (REPLACE/MIXED/APPEND). Defense inventory: claim.py, check.sh guards, close_lane.py. Stale-write detector tool built and wired into pre-commit.
expect: FM-19 at 29% collision rate concentrated in few files. claim.py is advisory-only. Collision rate reducible to <10% with write-intent declaration.

defense_inventory.swiss_cheese_gap: 2 automated layers needed for general case

fix_implemented.detection_method: For each staged high-contention file, checks if another session modified it since this session's last commit. APPEND/MIXED: content-loss detection (compares HEAD lines with staged). REPLACE: warns about full overwrite.

findings.surface_concentration: 74.5% of contention in 5 files. NEXT.md alone accounts for 34.7% (60/173 events). Mitigation targeting top 5 files covers 3/4 of collision risk.
findings.defense_gap: General case has 0 automated layers (Swiss Cheese requires >=2). Only two narrow subcases have 1 layer each (lesson collision, lane closure). claim.py is voluntary and L-601 predicts decay. 29% collision rate is aggregate; REPLACE-mode files like maintenance-outcomes.json have effectively ~44% per-commit collision rate.
findings.risk_bimodality: Contention is bimodal: REPLACE-mode JSON files (full overwrite, high data loss) vs APPEND-mode markdown files (git merge usually handles). The stale-write detector differentiates: BLOCK for content loss, WARN for overwrites.
findings.structural_fix: stale_write_check.py adds first automated detection layer for FM-19 general case. Pre-commit BLOCK when content loss is detected in APPEND/MIXED files. This raises FM-19 from 0→1 automated layers. Swiss Cheese requires 2 — second layer recommendation: PostToolUse hook that checks HEAD before each Edit/Write on high-contention files.

claim_py_advisory.actual: purely advisory, not wired into any enforcement

reducible_to_10pct.actual: first automated layer added but needs prospective measurement over 20 sessions to verify reduction

actual: FM-19 collision surface confirmed: 74.5% in 5 files, 0 automated defenses. Built stale_write_check.py (content-loss BLOCK + concurrent-modification WARN) and wired into check.sh pre-commit. FM-19 goes from 0→1 automated layers. Needs second layer (PostToolUse hook) for Swiss Cheese compliance.
diff: Expected reducible to <10% — can't measure yet (needs prospective tracking). All other predictions confirmed. Surprise: NEXT.md alone is 34.7% of contention — single-file targeting would be highest ROI.
